content:
CRITICAL FIRST STEP:
1. Read the LIVE DELEGATION SNAPSHOT included in this prompt. Treat it as the authoritative assignment for this round.
2. If you need the stored context verbatim or want to inspect changes, use context_read(action="read", name="Delegation Instructions") before proceeding.
3. Do NOT proceed based on memory from earlier rounds when the live snapshot is present.

GENERATION MODE:
IMPORTANT: The ontology has exactly ONE namespace: <https://example.org/ns/riepr#>. Type every new instance's rdf:type with an IRI in this EXACT namespace (e.g. <https://example.org/ns/riepr#YourClass>, or declare `@prefix : <https://example.org/ns/riepr#>` yourself and use `:YourClass`). Do NOT bind `:`/`ex:` or any other prefix to a different namespace, and do NOT invent any other namespace either - not a generic placeholder, not a variant of this one, not a per-topic or per-class namespace of your own. Every class lives in the ONE namespace above, with no exceptions.
RICHNESS PROFILE: RICH
- Generate required SHACL triples and substantial realistic optional enrichment (context, relationships, provenance, temporal details).
- You may invent new predicates for useful domain facts when existing ontology predicates are insufficient. Keep them consistent and reusable.

- If NO instances of your assigned target class exist, CREATE them from scratch.
- Generate realistic instances that satisfy SHACL constraints.
- Type every new instance with the EXACT target class URI from the SHACL/Ontology context (the
  same namespace as the shapes' sh:targetClass). Do NOT invent a different or placeholder
  namespace, even one that looks plausible or related — an instance typed in the wrong namespace
  will not match any shape, so it will never actually be validated.
- A shape's own name is the name of a validation rule, not necessarily a class - never assume it
  is one. Always look up your assigned shape's sh:targetClass property in the SHACL context and
  use THAT value's exact URI as the rdf:type for new instances.
- Reuse existing ontology/vocabulary predicates; do not invent new ones (see delegated shape's
  sh:path values and the ontology context for what already exists).
- Look up your assigned target class's `hydra:search [ a hydra:IriTemplate ; hydra:template "..." ;
  hydra:mapping [...] ]` in the Ontology context and mint new instance IRIs by substituting real
  values into that exact template. Only fall back to another URI pattern/convention if the class
  has no hydra:search template. A resolved template contains `/` in its path, so write it as a
  full IRI in angle brackets (`<...>`) - NEVER as a `prefix:LocalName` prefixed name, since a
  prefixed name's local part cannot contain an unescaped `/` and will fail to parse.
- Create required supporting resources when needed.
- Mint a named IRI for every instance you create. Do NOT use blank nodes (`[ ... ]` or `_:id`) for primary domain entities; reserve them for genuinely anonymous auxiliary structures only (e.g. RDF list cells).
- Do NOT include `#` comments in the Turtle you write (no worker/pool/range bookkeeping notes) — only RDF triples.

QUALITY-FIRST RULES:
- Work ONLY on your assigned target class from delegation context.
- Make minimum changes required for conformance.
- Do not modify other workers' target classes.
- Do NOT edit unrelated resources just to reduce global violation count.

VALIDATION LOOP:
- After changes, run shacl_validator(source="store").
- Fix only violations tied to your assigned shape(s)/focus nodes.
- If unrelated violations remain, report and stop.
- If there are no violations for your assigned shape/focus nodes in this pass, do not write triples and report VERIFIED_NO_CHANGE.
- If shacl_validator reports unrecognized predicates/classes on subjects you just created, remove
  those specific triples with triplestore_remove and re-add them using only predicates/classes
  from the delegated shape's sh:path values or the ontology context. Do not report BLOCKED for
  vocabulary you invented yourself - fix it and re-validate instead.

CHANGE SAFETY:
- Prefer additive fixes over destructive removals.
- Remove triples only when required to resolve a specific violation.
- Keep URI/prefix usage consistent with existing data.



DONE CRITERIA:
- At least one valid instance of your assigned target class exists (or more if cardinality requires).
- Assigned shape(s) have no remaining violations.
- Required properties/cardinalities are satisfied.

MANDATORY REPORTING (TOOL, NOT FREE TEXT):
- You MUST call:
  worker_progress(
    status=<CREATED|FIXED|VERIFIED_NO_CHANGE|BLOCKED>,
    target_shape=<shape name>,
    target_class=<class>,
    changed_triples_count=<integer>,
    created_or_updated_subjects=<comma-separated URIs or NONE>,
    validation_result=<CONFORMS|NON_CONFORMS>,
    remaining_issues=<short text or NONE>
  )
- Then provide a brief human summary (1-3 lines) consistent with the tool payload.


Assignment context: shapes 4-5 (worker 3 of 4). Follow your delegated task exactly (generate or fix as instructed). Use triplestore_add/remove/read and validation tools as needed.

LIVE DELEGATION SNAPSHOT (authoritative for this round; use this even if you do not call context_read):
TASK: Process ProcessShape (class :Process) and MeasurementInstrumentShape (class :MeasurementInstrument). CREATE new instances for both classes.

IRI PATTERNS:
- Process (from hydra:search): <https://example.org/id/proces/{localId}/{issued}/{created}> a :Process ; ...
- MeasurementInstrument (NO hydra:search - use default prefix): <https://example.org/ns/riepr#MeasurementInstrument-{uuid}> a :MeasurementInstrument ; ...

SUBSTITUTE real values for {localId} or {uuid}, {issued} (xsd:date), {created} (xsd:dateTime). Use xsd:dateTime format "2024-01-15T10:00:00" for created and xsd:date format "2024-01-15" for issued.

MANDATORY SHACL CONSTRAINTS (minCount=1):
- Process: rdfs:label, dc:created, dc:modified, adms:status
  Optional: dc:issued, dc:valid, dc:type, pplan:hasInputVar, pplan:hasOutputVar, ssn:implementedBy, :section, prov:wasRevisionOf, sosa:isHostedBy (:Installation or :Process)
- MeasurementInstrument: rdfs:label, dc:issued, dc:created, dc:modified, adms:status, sosa:isHostedBy (:OperationLocation)
  Optional: ogc:hasGeometry, dc:type, dc:valid, ssn:hasSubSystem, prov:wasRevisionOf, adms:identifier, ssn:hasDeployment

EXAMPLE Process:
<https://example.org/id/proces/procs-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> a :Process ;
  rdfs:label "Glass Melting Process"@en ;
  dc:created "2024-01-15T10:00:00"^^xsd:dateTime ;
  dc:modified "2024-01-15T10:00:00"^^xsd:dateTime ;
  adms:status :actief .

EXAMPLE MeasurementInstrument:
<https://example.org/ns/riepr#MeasurementInstrument-inst-001> a :MeasurementInstrument ;
  rdfs:label "Temperature Sensor TS-001"@en ;
  dc:issued "2024-01-15"^^xsd:date ;
  dc:created "2024-01-15T10:00:00"^^xsd:dateTime ;
  dc:modified "2024-01-15T10:00:00"^^xsd:dateTime ;
  adms:status :actief ;
  sosa:isHostedBy <https://example.org/id/exploitatielocatie/geo-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> .

PREDICATES MUST come from ontology/SHACL context only - never invent predicates. Each triplestore_add is validated as standalone Turtle.

REPORT: worker_progress(status, target_shape, target_class, changed_triples_count, validation_result, remaining_issues)

If this live snapshot differs from your memory of earlier rounds, follow this snapshot. Use context_read to inspect the stored context verbatim when needed.
context_reader({"action":"read","name":"Delegation Instructions"})
triplestore_read({"mode":"count"})
{"message":"The shared triple store contains 0 triples.","count":0,"status":"success"}

The shared triple store is empty. I need to create new instances for both :Process and :MeasurementInstrument classes. Let me first check the full context to understand available vocabulary (prefixes, predicates) before creating instances.
context_reader({"action":"read","name":"Ontology Context"})
{"error":"Context with name 'Ontology Context' not found"}
context_reader({"action":"list"})

{"changed_count":4,"contexts":[{"changed":true,"name":"example file","type":"text/plain","size":613},{"changed":true,"name":"Ontology","type":"text/turtle","size":100053},{"changed":true,"name":"Ontology Summary","type":"text/plain","size":1465},{"changed":true,"name":"Worker Progress Report","type":"text/plain","size":0},{"changed":false,"name":"Delegation Instructions","type":"text/plain","size":2110}],"message":"There are 4 changed context(s) since your last query","count":5}
context_reader({"action":"read_chunk","length":30000.0,"name":"Ontology","start":0.0})
The ontology is large. Let me continue reading to find :Process and :MeasurementInstrument definitions, as well as the :actief status:
context_reader({"action":"read_chunk","length":30000.0,"name":"Ontology","start":30000.0})
I found the :Process class definition with its hydra:search template. Now let me continue to find :MeasurementInstrument and :actief status:
context_reader({"action":"read_chunk","length":30000.0,"name":"Ontology","start":60000.0})

I found the :MeasurementInstrument class definition. It has no hydra:search (as stated), so I'll use the default pattern: `<https://example.org/ns/riepr#MeasurementInstrument-{uuid}>`. 

I also found :actief status in the ontology. Now let me read more to get complete context about other predicates used and SHACL shapes:
context_reader({"action":"read_chunk","length":30000.0,"name":"Ontology","start":90000.0})
Excellent! I now have all the necessary information. Let me check for SHACL shapes context as well:
context_reader({"action":"list"})

{"changed_count":0,"contexts":[{"changed":false,"name":"example file","type":"text/plain","size":613},{"changed":false,"name":"Ontology","type":"text/turtle","size":100053},{"changed":false,"name":"Ontology Summary","type":"text/plain","size":1465},{"changed":false,"name":"Worker Progress Report","type":"text/plain","size":0},{"changed":false,"name":"Delegation Instructions","type":"text/plain","size":2110}],"message":"No changes since last query","count":5}
context_reader({"action":"read_chunk","length":40000.0,"name":"example file","start":0.0})

{"has_more":false,"type":"text/plain","content":"MyGlassFactory is a glass factory that produces glass. They have a factory (Operation) that deploys
multiple systems. This Operation is located on a location (platform).

The factory implements a process that is subdivided into multiple steps that describe the process of producing glass. A process
can be subdivided into other processes.

An emission point is a point where emissions are released into the environment. A proces may be implemented by an
emission point (or other system).

Multiple versions of the factory (operation) exist. Versions may have an overlap in systems, processes but also differences.","chunk_start":0,"total_length":613,"chunk_end":613,"name":"example file"}
Now I have all the information I need. Let me verify if there are any existing instances of :Process and :MeasurementInstrument:
triplestore_read({"mode":"query","predicate":"http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type"})

{"triples":["<https://example.org/ns/riepr#Filter-filt-001> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#Filter> ."],"status":"success","message":"Query returned 1 triples from the shared store.","count":1}

Good! There is one triple in the store for a :Filter instance, but no :Process or :MeasurementInstrument instances exist yet. I need to create new instances for both classes.

Based on my analysis:

**Task:** Create new instances for :Process and :MeasurementInstrument

**IRI Patterns:**
- Process (from hydra:search): `<https://example.org/id/proces/{localId}/{issued}/{created}>`
- MeasurementInstrument (no hydra:search): `<https://example.org/ns/riepr#MeasurementInstrument-{uuid}>`

**Mandatory SHACL Constraints for :**Process:** rdfs:label, dc:created, dc:modified, adms:status
**Mandatory for :MeasurementInstrument:** rdfs:label, dc:issued, dc:created, dc:modified, adms:status, sosa:isHostedBy

**Statuses available:** :actief (Active)

Let me create one instance of each class with realistic data, then validate:
{"error":"Invalid TURTLE data: [line: 33, col: 5 ] Undefined prefix: ssa"}
I made a typo - `ssa` should be `ssn`. Let me fix that:
{"total_triples":102,"triples_added":14,"message":"Added 14 triples. Shared store now contains 102 triples.","status":"success"}
The triples were added successfully. Now let me validate the store:
shacl_validator({"source":"store"})
triplestore_read({"mode":"query","subject":"https://example.org/id/proces/procs-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00"})

{"triples":["<https://example.org/id/proces/procs-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#Process> .","<https://example.org/id/proces/procs-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label> \"Glass Melting Process\" .","<https://example.org/id/proces/procs-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://purl.org/dc/terms/created> \"2024-01-15T10:00:00\" .","<https://example.org/id/proces/procs-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://purl.org/dc/terms/modified> \"2024-01-15T10:00:00\" .","<https://example.org/id/proces/procs-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/ns/adms#status> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#actief> .","<https://example.org/id/proces/procs-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://purl.org/dc/terms/issued> \"2024-01-15\" .","<https://example.org/id/proces/procs-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/ns/ssn/implementedBy> <https://example.org/id/uitvoering/exploitatie-procs-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> ."],"status":"success","message":"Query returned 7 triples from the shared store.","count":7}

I see the ssn:implementedBy link to the Operation which is causing issues. Let me remove it and also check the language tag for rdfs:label - I notice the label doesn't have "@en" suffix! Let me fix both:
triplestore_remove({"subject":"https://example.org/id/proces/procs-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00","predicate":"http://www.w3.org/ns/ssn/implementedBy"})
{"triples_removed":1,"total_triples":80,"message":"Removed 1 triples (including any orphaned blank nodes). Shared store now contains 80 triples.","status":"success"}
Now let me update the Process instance with proper @en language tag on rdfs:label:

{"duplicates_ignored":6,"warning":"⚠️ 6 duplicate triples were ignored (already exist in shared store). Another worker may have already added them. Check triplestore_read to see existing data.","total_triples":91,"duplicate_triples":["<https://example.org/id/proces/procs-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/ns/adms#status> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#actief>","<https://example.org/id/proces/procs-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://purl.org/dc/terms/issued> \"2024-01-15\"","<https://example.org/id/proces/procs-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://purl.org/dc/terms/modified> \"2024-01-15T10:00:00\"","<https://example.org/id/proces/procs-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://purl.org/dc/terms/created> \"2024-01-15T10:00:00\"","<https://example.org/id/proces/procs-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label> \"Glass Melting Process\"","<https://example.org/id/proces/procs-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#Process>"],"triples_added":0,"message":"Added 0 new triples, ignored 6 duplicates. Shared store now contains 91 triples.","status":"success"}
The triples already existed but I didn't add the @en language tag to the label. Let me remove just the label and re-add it with proper language tagging:
triplestore_remove({"subject":"https://example.org/id/proces/procs-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00","predicate":"http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label"})
{"triples_removed":1,"total_triples":90,"message":"Removed 1 triples (including any orphaned blank nodes). Shared store now contains 90 triples.","status":"success"}
Now let me add everything again properly:

{"duplicates_ignored":5,"warning":"⚠️ 5 duplicate triples were ignored (already exist in shared store). Another worker may have already added them. Check triplestore_read to see existing data.","total_triples":91,"duplicate_triples":["<https://example.org/id/proces/procs-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/ns/adms#status> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#actief>","<https://example.org/id/proces/procs-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://purl.org/dc/terms/issued> \"2024-01-15\"","<https://example.org/id/proces/procs-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://purl.org/dc/terms/modified> \"2024-01-15T10:00:00\"","<https://example.org/id/proces/procs-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://purl.org/dc/terms/created> \"2024-01-15T10:00:00\"","<https://example.org/id/proces/procs-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#Process>"],"triples_added":1,"message":"Added 1 new triples, ignored 5 duplicates. Shared store now contains 91 triples.","status":"success"}

Now I have added the label with "@en". Now let me check what's wrong with :Process and MeasurementInstrument shapes more carefully by reading the current validation results for just my created entities:
shacl_validator({"source":"store"})

I see the issue now. Looking at violations for my created instances:

1. **For :Process:** "ClassConstraint[<http://www.w3.org/ns/adms#Status>]: Expected class ... for <https://example.org/ns/riepr#actief>"
2. **For :MeasurementInstrument-inst-001:** ClassConstraint for both OperationLocation and adms:status

The issue is that when SHACL validates, it's saying the object should be of type `<http://www.w3.org/ns/adms#Status>` but my value `:actief` resolves to something else or there's a naming conflict. Looking at the ontology, I see:
```
:actief rdf:type skos:Concept , adms:Status ;
```
So :actief is indeed typed as adms:Status. But the predicate prefix declarations in my turtle must show this correctly.

Look at the error message more carefully - it's expecting a value that IS `adms:Status` but I have `:actief`. This could mean there's a namespace issue where `:` is not resolving to `https://example.org/ns/riepr#` for SHACL validation purposes. However, looking at my triples read earlier, they were stored correctly:
```
<https://example.org/id/proces/procs-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/ns/adms#status> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#actief> .
```

So the triples are correct. The issue might be that my created linked nodes (like :Operator-op-999, :Filter-new-001) have missing properties and need to be fixed OR I should remove them too if they're pre-existing with violations. Looking at the context: these appear to be OTHER workers' data that has its own violations.

The specific violations relevant to my created instances are:
- `:MeasurementInstrument-inst-001` linked to `sosa:isHostedBy <https://example.org/id/exploitatielocatie/geo-001/...>` saying "Expected class :<https://example.org/ns/riepr#OperationLocation>"

Wait, that's a problem with the OperationLocation URI I linked to. Let me query what type that node has:
triplestore_read({"mode":"query","subject":"https://example.org/id/exploitatielocatie/geo-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00"})
{"triples":[],"status":"success","message":"Query returned 0 triples from the shared store.","count":0}

The OperationLocation node I referenced doesn't exist in the store! That's why SHACL is complaining - the `sosa:isHostedBy` predicate points to a resource that has no type declaration. This is NOT about my MeasurementInstrument being wrong, it's about the linked target not existing properly.

Let me check if OperationLocation nodes exist:
triplestore_read({"mode":"query","predicate":"http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type"})

{"triples":["<https://example.org/ns/riepr#Operator-Glass-Corp> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#Operator> .","<null> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <http://www.w3.org/ns/locn#Address> .","<https://example.org/id/emissiepunt/emis-002/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#Emissionpunt> .","<https://example.org/ns/riepr#Filter-new-001> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#Filter> .","<null> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#Geometry> .","<null> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <http://www.w3.org/ns/locn#Address> .","<https://example.org/id/meetpunt/meet-uuid-mp-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#MeasurementPoint> .","<https://example.org/id/Installation/install-002/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#Installation> .","<https://example.org/id/emissiepunt/emis-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#Emissionpunt> .","<https://example.org/id/Installation/install-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#Installation> .","<null> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#Geometry> .","<https://example.org/id/onttrekkingspunt/ont-uuid-wp-002/2024-01-16/2024-01-16T11:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#WithdrawalPoint> .","<https://example.org/ns/riepr#Operator-Cement-NV> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#Operator> .","<https://example.org/id/exploitatielocatie/geo-cement-mechelen/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#OperationLocation> .","<https://example.org/id/exploitatielocatie/geo-glass-antwerp/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#OperationLocation> .","<https://example.org/id/proces/procs-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#Process> .","<null> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <http://www.w3.org/ns/locn#Address> .","<https://example.org/ns/riepr#Operator-op-999> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#Operator> .","<https://example.org/ns/riepr#MeasurementInstrument-inst-001> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#MeasurementInstrument> .","<https://example.org/id/onttrekkingspunt/ont-uuid-wp-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#WithdrawalPoint> .","<https://example.org/id/meetpunt/meet-uuid-mp-002/2024-01-16/2024-01-16T11:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#MeasurementPoint> .","<https://example.org/ns/riepr#Operator-op-001> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#Operator> ."],"status":"success","message":"Query returned 22 triples from the shared store.","count":22}

Good! I can see that OperationLocation nodes exist:
- `<https://example.org/id/exploitatielocatie/geo-cement-mechelen/...>` 
- `<https://example.org/id/exploitatielocatie/geo-glass-antwerp/...>`

But NOT the one I referenced (`geo-001`). Since the store is shared across workers and there must be some pre-existing data with violations, my `sosa:isHostedBy` link to a non-existent node is causing issues.
